1 January 2025 – 20 March 2026 | |

(born 29 September 1994) is a Brazilian lawyer and politician currently serving as [mayor](/wiki/List_of_mayors_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "List of mayors of Rio de Janeiro") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), since 2026.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is a member of the [Social Democratic Party](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_(Brazil,_2011) "Social Democratic Party (Brazil, 2011)") (PSD).[[2]](#cite_note-2) He served as vice-mayor of Rio de Janeiro from 2025 to 2026, as the mayor’s chief of staff from 2023 to 2024[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) and as secretary of the environment from 2021 to 2023 during the mayoralty of [Eduardo Paes](/wiki/Eduardo_Paes "Eduardo Paes").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

["Paes deixa a Prefeitura do Rio com promessa quebrada, linguagem bolsonarista e apoio a Lula"](https://www1.folha.uol.com.br/poder/2026/03/paes-deixa-a-prefeitura-do-rio-com-promessa-quebrada-linguagem-bolsonarista-e-apoio-a-lula.shtml). *Folha de S.Paulo* (in Brazilian Portuguese). 2026-03-19. Retrieved 2026-03-20.

["Paes já tem data para filiar seus aliados ao PSD em busca de meta ousada"](https://veja.abril.com.br/politica/paes-ja-tem-data-para-filiar-seus-aliados-ao-psd-em-busca-de-meta-ousada/). *[VEJA](/wiki/VEJA "VEJA")* (in Brazilian Portuguese). Retrieved 7 October 2024.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mayor of Rio de Janeiro since 2026

In this [Portuguese name](/wiki/Portuguese_name "Portuguese name"), the first or maternal [family name](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is *Cavaliere* and the second or paternal family name is *Gonçalves Pinto*.

|  | |

**Eduardo Cavaliere Gonçalves Pinto** (born 29 September 1994) is a Brazilian lawyer and politician currently serving as [mayor](/wiki/List_of_mayors_of_Rio_de_Janeiro "List of mayors of Rio de Janeiro") of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), since 2026.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He is a member of the [Social Democratic Party](/wiki/Social_Democratic_Party_(Brazil,_2011) "Social Democratic Party (Brazil, 2011)") (PSD).[[2]](#cite_note-2) He served as vice-mayor of Rio de Janeiro from 2025 to 2026, as the mayor’s chief of staff from 2023 to 2024[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) and as secretary of the environment from 2021 to 2023 during the mayoralty of [Eduardo Paes](/wiki/Eduardo_Paes "Eduardo Paes").[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

In October 2021, he was the Brazilian delegate to the [COP26](/wiki/2021_United_Nations_Climate_Change_Conference "2021 United Nations Climate Change Conference"),[[8]](#cite_note-8) and later, in June 2022, to Stockholm+50,[[9]](#cite_note-9) organized by the Swedish government and hosted by the [United Nations General Assembly](/wiki/United_Nations_General_Assembly "United Nations General Assembly").

In [2022](/wiki/2022_Rio_de_Janeiro_gubernatorial_election "2022 Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial election"), Cavaliere was elected a state deputy for the state of [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro_(state) "Rio de Janeiro (state)"), with 33,688 votes.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

In [2024](/wiki/2024_Rio_de_Janeiro_mayoral_election "2024 Rio de Janeiro mayoral election"), he was elected to become the vice-mayor of the city of Rio de Janeiro, winning in the first round as part of the ticket led by incumbent Eduardo Paes. They won against the second place ticket, led by [Alexandre Ramagem](/wiki/Alexandre_Ramagem "Alexandre Ramagem") and [Índia Armelau](/wiki/%C3%8Dndia_Armelau "Índia Armelau"), by a 30-point margin. He took office on 1 January 2025.[[11]](#cite_note-11) He took office as mayor on 20 March 2026 after Eduardo Paes resigned to [run for governor](/wiki/2026_Rio_de_Janeiro_gubernatorial_election "2026 Rio de Janeiro gubernatorial election").[[12]](#cite_note-12)[[13]](#cite_note-13)
